title: missing dependency ?
body:
### Issue Description

not sure if this belongs in the present repo, but

after upgrading to fedora39, which comes with python-3.12, I notice there seems to be a missing dependency to the `rich` package

### Steps to reproduce the issue

Steps to reproduce the issue
1. install a fresh server under fedora39, with python and pip
2. install podman using `pip install podman`
3. import podman from python

```
# python
Python 3.12.0 (main, Oct  2 2023, 00:00:00) [GCC 13.2.1 20230918 (Red Hat 13.2.1-3)] on linux
Type "help", "copyright", "credits" or "license" for more information.
>>> import podman
Traceback (most recent call last):
  File "<stdin>", line 1, in <module>
  File "/usr/local/lib/python3.12/site-packages/podman/__init__.py", line 7, in <module>
    from podman.client import PodmanClient, from_env
  File "/usr/local/lib/python3.12/site-packages/podman/client.py", line 14, in <module>
    from podman.domain.containers_manager import ContainersManager
  File "/usr/local/lib/python3.12/site-packages/podman/domain/containers_manager.py", line 8, in <module>
    from podman.domain.containers import Container
  File "/usr/local/lib/python3.12/site-packages/podman/domain/containers.py", line 13, in <module>
    from podman.domain.images_manager import ImagesManager
  File "/usr/local/lib/python3.12/site-packages/podman/domain/images_manager.py", line 9, in <module>
    from rich.progress import Progress, TextColumn, BarColumn, TaskProgressColumn, TimeRemainingColumn
ModuleNotFoundError: No module named 'rich'
```

in my own env I see this

```bash
# pip show podman
Name: podman
Version: 4.8.0.post1
Summary: Bindings for Podman RESTful API
Home-page: https://github.com/containers/podman-py
Author: Brent Baude, Jhon Honce
Author-email: jhonce@redhat.com
License: Apache-2.0
Location: /usr/local/lib/python3.12/site-packages
Requires: pyxdg, requests, urllib3
Required-by: nbhosting
```


### Describe the results you received

Describe the results you received

```
ModuleNotFoundError: No module named 'rich'
```

### Describe the results you expected

Describe the results you expected

I should not have to care about podman's dependencies

### podman info output

```yaml
If you are unable to run podman info for any reason, please provide the podman version, operating system and its version and the architecture you are running.


root@thermals ~ (master) # podman info
ERRO[0000] graphroot must be set
```

```
# cat /etc/fedora-release
Fedora release 39 (Thirty Nine)

# python --version
Python 3.12.0

# rpm -q python3
python3-3.12.0-1.fc39.x86_64

# uname -a
Linux thermals 6.6.4-200.fc39.x86_64 containers/podman#1 SMP PREEMPT_DYNAMIC Sun Dec  3 18:13:11 UTC 2023 x86_64 GNU/Linux
```
```


### Podman in a container

No

### Privileged Or Rootless

None

### Upstream Latest Release

Yes

### Additional environment details

Additional environment details

for the record this is a box that was running fine under fedora-38 Python3.11 and the symptom appeared after a system upgrade

### Additional information

Additional information like issue happens only occasionally or issue happens with a particular architecture or on a particular setting

comments.body:
This is a podman-py issue not a podman issue.

@umohnani8 @jwhonce PTAL
